mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate profit downgrades and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of tight monetary policy signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, prompting central bank hawkishness that compresses equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during tightening cycles yields positive 20-day forward returns with a t-statistic > 2.0, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes in a dummy-variable regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, driving immediate positive market repricing and generating positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 202.0, real interest rates turn negative and central banks accelerate tightening, eroding future discounted cash flows and causing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 202.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 70% of cases, or the threshold effect is statistically insignificant in a quantile regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate hiring, which drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with an F-test p-value > 0.05 on the joint significance.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market peaks, a sudden drop in housing starts precedes a broader consumer spending contraction, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during housing peaks do not lead to negative 5-day returns, with the predictive coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.
